pain_point: [Toxicity — Mental Health] A significant number of physicians suffer from burnout for a variety of reasons, so it is hard to determine how many leave the profession over documentation.
[Toxicity — Mental Health] The statistics quantifying burnout though are really huge.
[Toxicity — Mental Health] The studying for the entrance exams itself makes many depressed/burnt out/unhealthy.
[Toxicity — Mental Health] The reasons for stress include financial issues, personal and professional conflicts, work related burnout.
[Toxicity — Mental Health] But as time passed, I realized that depression and emotional burnout are silently affecting so many medicos around me.
[Toxicity — Mental Health] It starts with ragging in first year and for some of us who are extra sensitive, it might be really stressful or torturous at times.
